item.title: Revised Outsourcing Rulebook Module – Cloud Services

obligations.description: Cloud outsourcing agreements must include mandatory data residency clauses specifying UAE jurisdiction.
obligations.gap: Outsourcing policy references data protection and exit provisions but does not mandate geographic data residency.

obligations.description: Right-to-audit must be contractually secured for all material cloud service providers.
obligations.evidence: Section 5: Outsourcing agreements must include audit rights.

obligations.description: Institution must assess and document cloud provider concentration risk annually.
obligations.gap: Policy does not mention cloud concentration risk or require periodic assessment of provider dependency.

obligations.description: Board must approve all material cloud outsourcing arrangements prior to go-live.
obligations.evidence: Section 5: Material outsourcing arrangements require prior board approval.

obligations.description: Gap assessment against updated module must be completed and remediated within 6 months.
obligations.gap: Policy has no mechanism for tracking regulatory module updates or mandating gap assessments on revision.

obligations.description: Cloud service agreements must include enforceable exit and transition provisions.
obligations.evidence: Section 5: outsourcing agreements covering … exit provisions.
obligations.gap: Policy references exit provisions generically but imposes no cloud-specific transition timelines or data return obligations.

obligations.description: Institution must maintain and test a business continuity plan covering cloud service disruption scenarios.
obligations.evidence: Section 3 (Operational Risk): BCPs must be documented and tested annually.
obligations.gap: BCP section does not explicitly require cloud-failure scenarios to be included as a tested disruption category.

obligations.description: Sub-contracting by cloud service providers must be disclosed to and approved by the institution.
obligations.gap: Policy has no provision requiring disclosure or approval of cloud sub-contractors (fourth-party risk).
